title: Hesperostipa curtiseta
taxonomy: Hesperostipa curtiseta is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It is further grouped into the subfamily Danthonioideae, which is a subfamily of grasses that is native to the Southern Hemisphere. The genus Hesperostipa is a group of Australian tussock grasses. Hesperostipa curtiseta is a species of tussock grass that is endemic to the southwestern regions of Western Australia. The species is often found growing in well-drained soils in woodland and heathland communities.
